{"step_by_step_thinking": "The question asks about the proper sequence when seating a final impression tray in dentistry. While the provided documents from MEDCORP and UMLS do not explicitly mention the seating order of a final impression tray, the context of dental impressions and tray placement is well-established in dental practice. In standard dental procedures, the final impression tray is typically seated posteriorly first. This is because the posterior region of the mouth has more stable and defined anatomy, and seating it first helps establish a proper foundation. Once the posterior portion is properly seated, the anterior portion can be adjusted to ensure accurate alignment and fit. This sequence ensures better accuracy and prevents displacement during the impression process. Therefore, based on standard dental protocols, the tray should be seated posteriorly first.", "answer_choice": "B"}
